questions.q: Which film production company did Raj Kapoor found in 1948?
questions.explanation: Raj Kapoor established R.K. Films in 1948, becoming one of the youngest studio founders in Indian cinema.

questions.q: By what title was Raj Kapoor popularly known in Hindi cinema?
questions.explanation: Raj Kapoor was widely celebrated as 'The Showman' of Indian cinema for his grand, emotive filmmaking.

questions.q: In which year was Raj Kapoor born?
questions.explanation: Raj Kapoor was born on 14 December 1924 in Peshawar, in present-day Pakistan.

questions.q: Which 1951 Raj Kapoor film became hugely popular across the Soviet Union?
questions.explanation: Awaara (1951), directed by and starring Raj Kapoor, became a massive hit across the USSR.

questions.q: Which actress was Raj Kapoor's most famous on-screen pairing in films like Awaara and Shree 420?
questions.explanation: Raj Kapoor and Nargis formed one of Hindi cinema's most iconic screen pairs in the late 1940s and 1950s.

questions.q: The famous 'common man' tramp persona Raj Kapoor adopted on screen was inspired by which Hollywood legend?
questions.explanation: Raj Kapoor modelled his endearing 'common man' tramp character on Charlie Chaplin's iconic figure.

questions.q: Which 1955 Raj Kapoor film featured the hit song 'Mera Joota Hai Japani'?
questions.explanation: 'Mera Joota Hai Japani', celebrating Indian identity, was a memorable song from Shree 420 (1955).

questions.q: Which acclaimed actor was Raj Kapoor's father?
questions.explanation: Raj Kapoor was the eldest son of the legendary actor and theatre pioneer Prithviraj Kapoor.

questions.q: In which year did Raj Kapoor die?
questions.explanation: Raj Kapoor died on 2 June 1988 in New Delhi at the age of 63.

questions.q: Which 1949 film, an early R.K. Films production starring Raj Kapoor and Nargis, was named after the rainy season?
questions.explanation: Barsaat (1949) was an early R.K. Films success and gave the studio its enduring rain-soaked logo.

questions.q: Which music director duo composed the iconic scores for Raj Kapoor's Awaara and Shree 420?
questions.explanation: The composer duo Shankar-Jaikishan scored many of Raj Kapoor's classic films, including Awaara and Shree 420.

questions.q: What was the title of the first film Raj Kapoor directed, released in 1948?
questions.explanation: Aag (1948) was Raj Kapoor's directorial debut, made when he was in his early twenties.

questions.q: Which playback singer provided the on-screen voice for most of Raj Kapoor's songs?
questions.explanation: Mukesh was Raj Kapoor's signature playback voice, so much so that Raj Kapoor called him his soul.

questions.q: In which city was Raj Kapoor born in 1924?
questions.explanation: Raj Kapoor was born in Peshawar, then part of British India and now in Pakistan.

questions.q: Which 1964 Raj Kapoor film, shot partly in Europe, told a love-triangle story involving an Air Force pilot?
options: Jis Desh Mein Ganga Behti Hai
questions.explanation: Sangam (1964), directed by and starring Raj Kapoor with Vyjayanthimala and Rajendra Kumar, was among the first Hindi films shot extensively abroad.

questions.q: Which 1973 Raj Kapoor production launched his son Rishi Kapoor and Dimple Kapadia as a teenage romance?
questions.explanation: Bobby (1973), directed by Raj Kapoor, launched Rishi Kapoor and Dimple Kapadia and became a major box-office hit.

questions.q: Which ambitious 1970 Raj Kapoor film, in which he played a clown, was a costly failure at release but later gained acclaim?
questions.explanation: Mera Naam Joker (1970), Raj Kapoor's deeply personal clown saga, flopped initially but is now regarded as a classic.

questions.q: Which 1960 Raj Kapoor film about a man among dacoits won the Filmfare Award for Best Film?
options: Jis Desh Mein Ganga Behti Hai
questions.explanation: Jis Desh Mein Ganga Behti Hai (1960), produced by R.K. Films and starring Raj Kapoor, won the Filmfare Best Film award.

questions.q: Raj Kapoor's iconic dream sequence song 'Ghar Aaya Mera Pardesi' appears in which of his films?
questions.explanation: The elaborate dream sequence 'Ghar Aaya Mera Pardesi' featured in Raj Kapoor's Awaara (1951).

questions.q: Which two younger brothers of Raj Kapoor also became prominent Hindi film actors?
questions.explanation: Raj Kapoor's younger brothers Shammi Kapoor and Shashi Kapoor were both major stars in their own right.

questions.q: In which 1951 song from Awaara does Raj Kapoor sing the title line declaring himself a vagabond?
questions.explanation: 'Awaara Hoon', sung by Mukesh, became Raj Kapoor's signature anthem and a global hit from Awaara.

questions.q: Which lyricists frequently collaborated with Raj Kapoor and Shankar-Jaikishan on R.K. Films songs?
questions.explanation: Lyricists Shailendra and Hasrat Jaipuri were core members of Raj Kapoor's R.K. Films creative team.

questions.q: Which civilian honour was conferred on Raj Kapoor by the Government of India in 1971?
questions.explanation: Raj Kapoor was awarded the Padma Bhushan, India's third-highest civilian honour, in 1971.

questions.q: For which year was Raj Kapoor honoured with the Dadasaheb Phalke Award, India's highest film honour?
questions.explanation: Raj Kapoor was the Dadasaheb Phalke Award recipient for the year 1987, presented shortly before his death in 1988.

questions.q: Which 1956 black-and-white film, an R.K. Films production, did Raj Kapoor act in but Sombhu Mitra and Amit Maitra direct, winning honours at Karlovy Vary?
questions.explanation: Jagte Raho (1956), starring Raj Kapoor, won the Crystal Globe at the Karlovy Vary International Film Festival.

questions.q: What was Raj Kapoor's full birth name?
questions.explanation: Raj Kapoor was born Ranbir Raj Kapoor; the Kapoor family later used the name Ranbir again for his grandson, actor Ranbir Kapoor.

questions.q: Which 1978 Raj Kapoor film, controversial for its treatment of beauty and sensuality, starred Zeenat Aman?
questions.explanation: Satyam Shivam Sundaram (1978), directed by Raj Kapoor, starred Zeenat Aman and stirred debate over its bold themes.

questions.q: Which 1985 film was the last feature directed by Raj Kapoor and launched Mandakini opposite Rajiv Kapoor?
questions.explanation: Ram Teri Ganga Maili (1985) was the last film Raj Kapoor directed and a major commercial success.

questions.q: Which 1949 Mehboob Khan film featured Raj Kapoor alongside Dilip Kumar and Nargis in a love triangle?
questions.explanation: Andaz (1949) paired Raj Kapoor with Dilip Kumar and Nargis in one of the era's most successful love triangles.

questions.q: Which posthumously completed film, started by Raj Kapoor and finished by his son Randhir Kapoor, was a cross-border love story released in 1991?
questions.explanation: Henna (1991), an India-Pakistan love story conceived by Raj Kapoor, was completed by Randhir Kapoor after his death.
